BACK
What is a tesseract?
What style of music is this? What's the right attitude to play it?

g.serif:id: Why did you make nested notation

g.text: Why did you make nested notation? What inspired its creation,
and who inﬂuenced its development?
Is there any kind of admin managing each session?
Is there an end to the piece?
What's the best gear to bring to a nested notation session?
I created nested notation out of a desire to express a more timeless music. Throughout my life I
have been challenged and inspired by Jesus Christ's experience with time.
As a young man, I encountered Jorge Luis Borges'
The Garden of Forking Paths
, where an
author creates a commentary on time speciﬁcally by omitting that word from his entire work.
Thanks to a chance gift from my mother, also as a young man, I heard the music of the Bayaka
people of west-central Africa, whose polyphonic choral culture seems to acknowledge time's
origin in eternity. For years I listened extensively to their music, pondering how such rich music
could spring from collective improvisation.
Later in life, I encountered the José Maceda:
A Concept of Time
which challenged my ideas
through its analysis of Southeast Asian gong music. Around that same time, I read Carlo
Rovelli's work
The Order of Time
, which discusses in a poetic fashion the profound locality and
relativity of time.
It became clear to me as I prayed that I should seek a way of relating musical ideas without
reliance on any kind of time axis (the Borges problem), and without using time-based divisions
of musical form integrally in the work.
This was José Maceda's insight speciﬁcally: that drone and melody are interchangeable, that
either rhythm or pitch* may serve as measure and formal structure in any musical culture based
in recognition of events rather than position in a Newtonian grid.
Over a period of a few years, nested notation took shape as a set of acknowledgements of
music's nature (and our present uses and needs for it) alongside the development of graphic
conventions, much like other notation systems developed over centuries by groups of musical
cultures.
In this perilous moment in human history, culturally and ecologically, we should be eager for
opportunities to think diﬀerently, to avoid war and extinction. Yet we often dig in our heels
more than ever. Nested notation is my personal way of saying, "there are other ways out there,
to do things; before we concern ourselves with the ultimate value of new paths, let's learn what
we can about them, and enjoy the process if at all possible."
*or texture, timbre, volume, signiﬁed meaning, degree of passion, etc., provided its integration
is holistic and consistent in the work and across an œuvre. This is Indigenous / Native wisdom.
